factors.name: Japan GDP beat
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP annualized 2.1% vs 1.7% expected, beating on growth. Positive for risk sentiment and USD weakness, but modest direct impact on NAS100.

factors.name: Iran geopolitical relief
factors.summary: Trump postponed scheduled Iran strike; Saudi/UAE/Qatar seeking nuclear deal. Eases oil risk and inflation expectations, reducing near-term recession probability (Goldman cut to 25% from 30%).

factors.name: Fed policy shift to hawkish (Warsh transition)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed chair; MUFG expects dollar extension and higher real yields. Goolsbee reinforced inflation 'front of mind' messaging. Markets pricing 50% chance of rate hike by year-end.

factors.name: Real yields and long-duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Warsh hawkishness signals sticky real yields; 10Y Treasury volatility and bond rout warnings from Morgan Stanley. Long-duration tech (NDX-heavy) vulnerable if real yields stay elevated or rise further.

factors.name: USD strength
factors.summary: Dollar posted best week since Iran conflict (up 1.4%) on hawkish Fed + hot inflation data. NDX has heavy international revenue exposure; stronger USD headwind for earnings translation.

factors.name: Morgan Stanley equity correction warning
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley warns bond rout could trigger meaningful correction if volatility persists, though bank raises S&P 500 target to 8,300. Suggests near-term chop risk even with bullish longer-term view.

factors.name: Inflation expectations (RBA, Japan GDP deflator)
factors.summary: RBA's Hunter warns of elevated risk of inflation expectations drifting higher from oil pass-through; Japan GDP deflator running 3.4% y/y (hot). Hawkish central bank bias emerging globally.

factors.name: Options positioning on NVDA / tech earnings
factors.summary: NVDA call volume outpaced puts >2x, but more calls traded at bid/below (likely sold, not bought). Positioning mixed; earnings anxiety evident ahead of Nvidia results.

factors.name: Price action: 4h +0.29%, 24h -0.17%
factors.summary: Minimal intraday move; mixed overnight action (Dow +, S&P/Nadaq -). Rebound off session lows on Iran news, but no conviction breakout. Suggests market still digesting conflicting forces.

factors.name: Tech mega-cap sentiment mixed
factors.summary: ServiceNow +1yr best day (software optimism), but Seagate weakness, Nvidia questions, and Musk/OpenAI verdict overhang. No consensus direction in semis or mega-cap growth.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two opposing macro headwinds: (1) Hawkish Fed transition (Warsh) + sticky real yields + strong dollar = bearish for long-duration growth multiples; (2) Iran geopolitical relief + Japan growth beat + recession odds falling = bullish risk sentiment. The index rebounded off session lows on Iran pause news, but lacks conviction (only +0.29% in 4h, -0.17% on 24h). Morgan Stanley warns of correction risk if bond volatility persists, signaling chop ahead of major tech earnings. Real yields, not nominal yields, are the critical variable for NDX valuation—and Warsh signals they'll stay elevated.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA earnings (TBD, likely late May — critical for semis & AI capex narrative)
FOMC minutes & upcoming rate decision (re-rate expectations on Warsh commentary)
Treasury yield moves (track 10Y & TIPS closely for real yield regime)
Google I/O AI showcase (May 20 — potential AI capex/cloud demand signaling)
US-Iran nuclear deal outcome (ongoing, impacts oil, inflation expectations)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran nuclear deal breaks down and oil spikes >$150/bbl, geopolitical premium reinstates and inflation expectations rise sharply—crushing long-duration equities and overriding Fed hawkishness relief. Conversely, if Warsh signals are misread as dovish by markets or CPI cools sharply, real yields could fall and NDX could re-rate higher. Major tech earnings disappointments or cloud capex guidance cuts would flip bias decisively bearish regardless of macro backdrop.
screening.entry_guidance: Market in chop zone (move underway but inconclusive). Avoid chasing; wait for clearer directional conviction on next 10Y yield move (track 4h close) or hold for NVDA/MSFT earnings catalyst. If resistance at 29100+ breaks on volume, re-assess bullish. If support 28800 breaks, confirm bear bias and short into 28600–28400 zone.

factors.name: US Treasury Yields / Real Yields
factors.summary: 10Y yields flirting with 2007 highs; bond rout persists as inflation concerns mount (AllianzGI warns markets may be overpricing inflation). Rising real yields compress long-duration tech multiples, headwind for NQ components.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Warsh Appointment
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signals hawkish shift on inflation; rate-hike expectations rising. MUFG notes dollar posted best week since Iran conflict partly on Warsh hawkish signal. Equity-negative dynamic.

factors.name: Inflation Data & Pass-Through Risk
factors.summary: RBA Assistant Governor Hunter warns elevated inflation expectations risk from Middle East energy shock; producer price pressures building in NZ/Australia. No fresh CPI/PCE yet, but expectations shifting higher.

factors.name: NVDA Earnings (Wed 20 May)
factors.summary: InvestingLive reports NVDA has moderately bearish event-risk bias (-3.4/+10 score); stock already made large move into earnings, tested resistance near $236.54 and failed to break. Earnings-day volatility risk.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran / Middle East)
factors.summary: Trump postponed Iran strike at Gulf leaders' request, reducing immediate tail risk. However, uncertainty remains 'very binary' per Bloomberg; oil fell 2% but energy volatility persists, keeping risk sentiment fragile.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD Index holds gains above 99.00; dollar posted strongest week in months on Warsh hawkish signal + inflation concerns. Strong dollar headwind for NQ international revenue exposure (~40% of mega-cap earnings from overseas).

factors.name: Tech Sentiment / Breadth
factors.summary: Korean stocks down 5% as chip slump fuels overheating concerns; weak breadth in high-flying names. Analyst caution on AI capex sustainability vs. current valuations.

factors.name: China Macro / Demand
factors.summary: Iron ore fell to 2-week low on China steel demand concerns; China resumed foreign inflows post-Iran outflow, but momentum fragile. Putin heading to Beijing for energy talks; geopolitical jostling unclear for tech supply chains.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.29% in 4h but -0.17% in 24h; micro-bounce into earnings. Move is marginal, no strong directional conviction yet. Prior read (2026-05-19 00:05) was NEUTRAL 5/10.

factors.name: Morgan Stanley Correction Warning
factors.summary: MS warns equities face first meaningful correction since late March if bond volatility persists; though MS itself raises S&P 500 target to 8300. Mixed signal but correction risk noted.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between a tightening Fed narrative (Warsh hawkish shift, rising real yields, inflation expectations drifting higher) and a mixed geopolitical backstop (Iran strike postponed, but energy volatility lingers). The massive bond rout and yield surge—especially in real rates—are direct headwinds for long-duration growth multiples. NVDA's moderately bearish earnings setup (large move into event, failed breakout near $236.54) adds single-name tail risk to the index 24h out. Sentiment is fragile; breadth concerns in tech (Korean chip stocks down 5%) suggest conviction is fading into the event cycle.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA earnings (Wed 20 May 2026) — single biggest catalyst; any miss or soft guidance 2-3% NQ swing risk
FOMC meeting / Fed communications — Warsh's first actions & tone-setting highly watched
US inflation data (CPI/PCE) — any hot print will spike yields further, crush growth multiples
Iran escalation / geopolitical resolution (oil/dollar feedback loops)
screening.risk_warning: If NVDA beats and provides strong forward guidance, or if Fed communication softens hawkish tone, NQ could rally 1-2% quickly. Conversely, if real yields spike further (e.g., CPI hot) or NVDA disappoints, -2 to -3% correction likely. Bond market stability is the linchpin; sustained >4.50% 10Y with climbing real yields = extended downside.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is pricing NVDA event risk; wait for post-earnings retest or a pullback to 28,800–28,900 support before considering longs. Shorts on fresh break below 28,800 with conviction only if yields climb >4.50% and stay there. Earnings day (Wed) = elevated chop; prefer entry after volatility settles.
